messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0060",
  "title": "以务实策略校准新品预期 瞻准生态位进行资源配置",
  "page_index": 19,
  "page_range": "19"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0061",
    "title": "图表类型",
    "page_range": "19",
    "summary": "This section covers the topic of chart types, specifically identifying \"折线图\" (line chart) as a listed category. It can answer questions regarding which chart types are referenced or included in the document. The excerpt does not contain dates, definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, or evidence types."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0062",
    "title": "数据趋势",
    "page_range": "19",
    "summary": "This section answers retrieval queries about quantitative data trends across ranked items (TOP1–TOP10) for the years 2023, 2024, and 2025, plus a modified 2023 scenario excluding the top \"super hit.\" It provides specific metric values at the TOP1 and TOP10 positions: 2023 declines from 85 to ~7; 2024 from ~30 to ~12; 2025 from ~40 to ~7; and the adjusted 2023 baseline from ~45 to ~6. The data corresponds to a line chart figure visualized with blue solid (2023), cyan solid (2024), green solid (2025), and blue dashed (2023 adjusted) lines. The core claim is a consistent gradual decline in values from the highest to the tenth rank across all periods. Evidence type: quantitative line chart metrics used to track year-over-year ranking distribution and the impact of outlier removal. No definitions or procedures are covered."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0063",
    "title": "具体数值",
    "page_range": "19",
    "summary": "This section provides structured quantitative data that can answer retrieval questions regarding:\n- **Topics & Metrics:** Ranked numerical values (TOP1–TOP10) for performance or volume indicators across specified years.\n- **Dates:** Data points for 2023, 2024, 2025, and a modified 2023 condition (\"after removing the TOP1 super hit\").\n- **Entities:** Ranked positions from TOP1 to TOP10.\n- **Values & Evidence Type:** Exact and approximate numerical figures (e.g., 2023 TOP1 = 85; all others marked \"约\"/approximate). Functions as a direct quantitative reference list.\n- **Absent Elements:** Contains no definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, or explicit measurement units.\nIt supports queries on year-over-year ranked metric comparisons, the numerical impact of excluding the top performer in 2023, and precise/approximate value lookups for each rank per timeframe."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0064",
    "title": "总结",
    "page_range": "19-1",
    "summary": "This section summarizes a chart analyzing yearly data trends (2023–2025) across rankings, specifically addressing the impact of the TOP1 \"super hit.\" It can answer retrieval questions regarding:\n- **Topics & Claims:** Year-over-year performance comparisons; the disproportionate influence of the TOP1 item on overall metrics; and how 2023 data shifts when the TOP1 super hit is excluded.\n- **Dates & Entities:** 2023, 2024, 2025; TOP1 ranking position; \"super hit\" (超级爆款) products; Diandian Data (点点数据).\n- **Metrics & Figures:** Comparative yearly data values, ranking-based fluctuations, and a chart visualizing these trends.\n- **Evidence Type:** Proprietary research and chart visualization sourced from Diandian Data.\nThe section does not contain definitions or procedural information."
  }
]

message.content: This section (Page 19) addresses the calibration of new product expectations and strategic resource allocation through quantitative analysis of ranked performance data. **Topics** covered include year-over-year trend analysis, the statistical impact of removing top-performing outliers, and data-driven resource positioning. **Entities** include ranked positions (TOP1–TOP10), "super hit" (超级爆款) products, and the analytics provider Diandian Data (点点数据). **Dates** span 2023, 2024, and 2025, alongside a modified 2023 baseline that excludes the TOP1 performer. **Figures** feature a line chart (折线图) visualizing these trends with distinct line styles (blue solid for 2023, cyan solid for 2024, green solid for 2025, and blue dashed for the adjusted 2023 scenario); no tables are included. **Claims** state that metric values consistently decline gradually from the highest to the tenth rank across all periods, and that the TOP1 "super hit" exerts a disproportionate influence on overall yearly distributions. **Metrics** consist of exact and approximate numerical values at the TOP1 and TOP10 positions (e.g., 2023 declines from 85 to ~7; 2024 from ~30 to ~12; 2025 from ~40 to ~7; adjusted 2023 from ~45 to ~6). **Evidence types** comprise quantitative line chart metrics and proprietary research data sourced from Diandian Data. The section does not contain definitions or procedures.
